channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Mandatory evacuations begin in Tampa Bay ahead of Milton
item.description: Ahead of Tropical Storm Milton's anticipated landfall as a major hurricane in Florida this week, mandatory evacuations in Tampa Bay have begun.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, NewsNation

item.title: A Pakistani separatist group claims bombing that killed 2 Chinese near Karachi airport
item.description: KARACHI, Pakistan (AP) — A Pakistani separatist group claimed responsibility for a late-night bombing that targeted a convoy with Chinese nationals outside the country's largest airport, killing two workers from China and wounding eight people, officials and the insurgent group said Monday.
item.dc:creator: ADIL JAWAD and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: President Biden approves Florida Emergency Declaration
item.description: President Joe Biden has declared a state of emergency in Florida and ordered Federal assistance to supplement State and local response efforts.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Major North Carolina newspaper knocks Trump over Helene response 'falsehoods'
item.description: A leading newspaper in North Carolina is blasting former President Donald Trump over what it calls his "falsehoods" about the government response to Hurricane Helene, which devastated large swaths of the state last week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New analysis suggests national debt could increase under Harris, but it would surge under Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — No one is likely to be happy with the projected higher deficits laid out in a new analysis of Kamala Harris' and Donald Trump's economic plans.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Miss Teen Rodeo Kansas, 19, killed in car crash; teammates injured
item.description: Emma Brungardt, 19, was killed in a single-vehicle crash that left several of her teammates injured on Friday in rural Thomas County, Kansas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tips, overtime, Social Security: A look at Donald Trump's no-tax pledges and what they might cost
item.description: Donald Trump has pledged to end taxes on everything from tips to Social Security and overtime pay if he's elected to the White House again. But he hasn't detailed how he would fund those ideas and avoid creating a huge budget shortfall, beyond arguing he will usher in an economic boom.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court won't hear arguments in Texas emergency abortion case
item.description: The Supreme Court decided not to hear arguments in a case involving Texas that could have provided an answer about whether a state abortion ban conflicts with a federal emergency care law.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump intends to use military to round up opponents: Michael Cohen
item.description: “The big warning that I want people to understand is when Donald Trump says something, stop sane washing it. Stop trying to make it into something which has some normalcy to it. What he says, he intends to do.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New film documents Oct. 7 attacks after first anniversary
item.description: Pierre Rehov, speaking from Ashdod, Israel, said he was disturbed by reactions that seemed to excuse the attack.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Israel intensifies bombardment of Gaza and southern Lebanon ahead of Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: A new round of airstrikes hit Beirut suburbs late Sunday as Israel intensified its bombardment of northern Gaza and southern Lebanon in a widening war with Iran-allied militant groups across the region. Palestinian officials said a strike on a mosque killed at least 19 people.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: North Carolina first responders exchange vows among chaos in Hurricane Helene aftermath
item.description: A North Carolina first responder couple honored their love for each other with a commitment ceremony on Oct. 2 after Hurricane Helene robbed them of their initial wedding plans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: See the 10 most – and least – valuable college majors: study
item.description: A new study from financial publisher Bankrate found that STEM college majors dominate the ranking of valuable fields of study – but money isn't the only consideration.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Tennessee nurse and his dog died trying to save a man from floods driven by Helene
item.description: “I’ve never had anyone risk their life for me,” David Boutin, the man Boone McCrary had set out to rescue said. “From what I hear that was the way he always been. He’s my guardian angel, that’s for sure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Nibi the 'diva' beaver won't be released, Massachusetts governor decides
item.description: The question of whether a 2-year-old beaver named Nibi can stay with the rescuers she has known since she was a baby or must be released into the wild has been resolved, thanks to the governor of Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Pro-Palestinian and pro-Israeli crowds rally across the world on the eve of Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Crowds were participating in pro-Palestinian and pro-Israeli protests and memorial events across Europe, North Africa and Asia on Sunday on the eve of the first anniversary of the Hamas attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for 5th consecutive year
item.description: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for the fifth year in a row, the retail giant recently announced in its holiday plans.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Can you stop calls and texts from political campaigns?
item.description: If you've received a political text or call recently, you may be wondering how to make it stop, or what you can do to prevent them in the future.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, NewsNation

item.title: Fires at home and abroad fuel anxieties in campaign homestretch
item.description: A series of turbulent events have left Democrats feeling hopeful but anxious a month out from November’s elections, with races for the House and White House too close to call.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Helene flooding in NC stirs yellow jacket swarms, prompting distribution of Benadryl and EpiPens
item.description: Swarms of stinging insects in North Carolina are swarming those trying to recover from the aftermath of Hurricane Helene, prompting the distribution of Benadryl and epinephrine injections.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Voters in North Carolina and Georgia have bigger problems than politics. Helene changed everything
item.description: VILAS, N.C. (AP) — Brad Farrington pulls over to grab a case of water bottles being passed out in Vilas, a small rural community tucked away in the Blue Ridge Mountains. He’s on his way to help a friend who lost much of what he owned when Hurricane Helene blew through last weekend.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here's what Spirit Halloween seeks in an 'ideal' retail location
item.description: Spirit Halloween made headlines earlier this week after firing back at "SNL" over a sketch that mocked the company's choice of retail locations.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Tren de Aragua gang members arrested in police raid at Texas apartment complex
item.description: San Antonio Police Chief William McManus confirmed four Tren de Aragua members were arrested during a raid on a vacant apartment complex.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Couple reunited after volunteer rescue pilot threatened with arrest in North Carolina
item.description: Jordan Seidhom, who decided to go help those impacted by Helene with his own helicopter on his own dime, was able to rescue Susan Coffey off a mountainside. But, before he could go back for her husband, Mike, and his own son and co-pilot, he was threatened with arrest.
item.dc:creator: Jody Barr, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: See the 10 most – and least – valuable college majors: study
item.description: A new study from financial publisher Bankrate found that STEM college majors dominate the ranking of valuable fields of study – but money isn't the only consideration.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: For small cities across Alabama with Haitian populations, Springfield is a cautionary tale
item.description: ENTERPRISE, Ala. (AP) — The transition from the bustling Port-au-Prince, Haiti, to a small Alabama city on the southernmost tip of the Appalachian mountain range was challenging for Sarah Jacques. But over the course of a year, the 22-year-old got used to the quiet and settled in.
item.dc:creator: SAFIYAH RIDDLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Affluent Virginia suburb rocked by love triangle murders as prosecutors highlight key evidence
item.description: Virginia prosecutors say blood-spatter analysis led to the arrest of Brendan Banfield in connection with the 2023 murders of his wife and an unknown man.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge rules Missouri sex offenders no longer need to post warning signs outside their homes on Halloween
item.description: A federal judge ruled that Missouri's sex offenders are no longer required to display "no candy" warning signs outside their homes on Halloween, arguing it is unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump urges his supporters to deliver victory in his return to scene of first assassination attempt
item.description: BUTLER, Pa. (AP) — Donald Trump returned on Saturday to the Pennsylvania fairgrounds where he was nearly assassinated in July, urging a large crowd to deliver an Election Day victory that he tied to his survival of the shooting.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, JILL COLVIN and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former Capitol Police officer recounts Jan. 6: 'It's hard to relive'
item.description: Former Capitol Hill Police Officer Winston Pingeon recalled the events of Jan. 6, 2021, when rioters breached the Capitol in an apparent attempt to stop Congress’s certification of the Electoral College results, in an interview with ABC News. "It's hard to relive and talk about moments like this.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Clayton Kershaw won't pitch for the Dodgers in playoffs after big toe injury fails to heal
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Clayton Kershaw, sidelined since late August with a toe injury, won’t pitch again this season for the Los Angeles Dodgers.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Russian prosecutors seek 7-year sentence for US man accused of fighting for Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian prosecutors asked for a seven-year sentence in the trial of a U.S. citizen accused of fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine against Russia, Russian news agencies reported Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Entire Chicago school board to resign over teachers union dispute with Dem mayor: 'Deeply alarming'
item.description: The entire Chicago Board of Education is resigning after reportedly declining to fire the Chicago Public Schools CEO during contract negotiations with the city's teachers union.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oklahoma defends Bibles-in-schools proposal after report that only Trump's might qualify
item.description: Oklahoma Education Superintendent Ryan Walters’s (R) office is defending the process for how the state will be selecting newly mandated Bibles in classrooms after a report was released that the criteria is so narrow that essentially no Bibles qualify — besides ones endorsed by former President Trump
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Hurricane Kirk strengthens into a Category 4 storm in the Atlantic
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Hurricane Kirk was a Category 4 major hurricane on Thursday, and waves from the system could cause life-threatening surf and rip current conditions this weekend along the U.S. East Coast as well as in Bermuda, the Greater Antilles and the Bahamas, forecasters said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Last pandemic-era student loan benefit ends even as borrowers struggle to make payments
item.description: The last of the pandemic-era student loan benefits are gone, winding down four years of additional help to borrowers that advocates say was never actually enough.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, ABC 4
